paper_id: Haridy et al. - 2021 - OntoUML Conceptual Model for Egyptian E-Government Student and Traveler Case Studies

models.model_label: OntoUML conceptual model of Egyptian E-Government - Student case study

evidence_quotes.quote: Conceptual models were developed to help stakeholders better understand and communicate about a system or domain.
The model has the potential to improve knowledge of crucial issues, particularly in Egypt's e-government development process.
This model enhances the understanding of important issues in eGovernment development process in Egypt.

goals.justification: Authors explicitly frame the conceptual model's purpose as improving understanding/knowledge of the Egyptian e-government domain, which is a domain-understanding goal.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is also expected to be used to assess the adoption of ICT in government institutions.
Moreover, it is expected to be used as an evaluation in government institutions.

uncertain_goals.reason: Assessment/evaluation use is stated only as future/expected use, not as an actualized purpose, so evidence is hypothetical.

evidence_quotes.quote: This model has been implemented using OLED [25]. It is a model-based environment that supports the task of formalization, implementation, verification and validation of OntoUML models.

uncertain_goals.reason: 'Implemented using OLED' refers to formalizing the model in a modeling environment rather than deploying a real-world system; no artifact/system generation is demonstrated.

models.model_label: OntoUML conceptual model of Egyptian E-Government - Traveler case study

evidence_quotes.quote: Conceptual models were developed to help stakeholders better understand and communicate about a system or domain.
The model has the potential to improve knowledge of crucial issues, particularly in Egypt's e-government development process.
This model enhances the understanding of important issues in eGovernment development process in Egypt.

goals.justification: The Traveler portion is presented as another fragment of the same OntoUML conceptual model whose stated purpose is to enhance domain understanding of the Egyptian e-government.

evidence_quotes.quote: It is also expected to be used to assess the adoption of ICT in government institutions.
Moreover, it is expected to be used as an evaluation in government institutions.

uncertain_goals.reason: Assessment/evaluation is described only as an expected future application, not something demonstrated in this paper.

evidence_quotes.quote: This model has been implemented using OLED [25]. It is a model-based environment that supports the task of formalization, implementation, verification and validation of OntoUML models.

uncertain_goals.reason: 'Implemented' here means formalized in the OntoUML editor rather than used to build/deploy a system; no downstream artifact generation is shown.
